note_on_counts: File/dir/LOC counts are no longer hand-maintained here. See docs/catalog/MODULE_REGISTRY.json (metrics block) generated by scripts/generate_catalog.py to keep numbers verifiable. NOTE: generate_catalog.py --check only validates registry structure, not that generated metrics/Markdown are fresh — see 2026-07-22 Codex review Medium Finding 6.

validation.python_tests: 65 passed (as of 2026-07-22; verify with `python -m pytest -q`, do not trust this number without rerunning)
validation.catalog_verify: PASS (python scripts/generate_catalog.py --check) - structural check only, does not catch metric/Markdown drift

cvf_enforcement.2026-07-22_correction: A prior version of this file said 'golden_vertical: Operational Event -> confirm' and listed 8 controls as enforced. An independent review (docs/decisions/EA_INDEPENDENT_REVIEW_2026-07-22_CODEX.md) found that label was overbroad: gate functions exist and are unit-tested (callable), but several are bypassable or not load-bearing in the real request path. See docs/cvf/CVF_CONTROL_MAPPING.md for the corrected, evidence-based status of each control.

notes: Zalo and WhatsApp are mock/conformance skeletons until official credentials and API approval are available.
Production hardening remains gated by Phase 5 acceptance evidence.
Persistence: SqlLedger (SQLite verified, PostgreSQL not yet run live) plus InMemoryLedger for tests/offline. See operations-ledger entry in the module catalog.
Do not treat this file as current without cross-checking docs/catalog/MODULE_CATALOG.md and SESSION/ACTIVE_SESSION_STATE.json - front-door drift was itself a finding of the 2026-07-22 review.
